place: Roslyn Heights New York, New York

facts.text: Roslyn Heights Historic District, bounded by Willis Avenue and the Long Island Rail Road station, was officially established in 1999.
The Bryant Library’s Local History Collection, founded in 1953 as the Grist Mill Historical Collection, preserves the heritage of Roslyn Heights and surrounding villages.
Interview recordings in the library’s Oral History Collection capture early‑20th‑century voices, including the “Everyday Voices” series and Christopher Morley’s “Memories of Morley.”
Roslyn station sits in the heart of Roslyn Heights just south of Lincoln Avenue, while Albertson station borders the hamlet north of I.U. Avenue.
Before adopting the name Roslyn in the 1840s, the area now known as Roslyn Heights was called Hempstead Harbor.
